meta.setting: Seasonal pressure across Jefferson roads, claims, timber camps, creek bottoms, ranch edges, and high ridges
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with changing road access, animal movement, labor cycles, supply strain, and weather-timed scenes

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Mining, logging, ranching, road travel, and changing working conditions around Whiskeytown.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Deer
research_grounding.use: Seasonal wildlife movement and sign across California habitats.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Seasonal Shifts region page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: H3 local marker line in the register of Snow Line, Runoff, Dust, and Smoke
square image reference immediately after the marker line
opening blockquote built as three short generated lines: seasonal object, weather or animal sign, plain table meaning
two compact paragraphs establishing seasons as travel clock, work calendar, wildlife movement, fire risk, shortage pressure, and rumor timer
closing blockquote that gives a generated seasonal sign and practical table meaning
generation_guidance: Generate seasons as concrete pressure: spring runoff, winter snow line, summer dust, autumn smoke, swollen creeks, dry wells, deer movement, logging windows, and claims that only open when roads do.
Give the table hooks around a pass closing early, supply arriving late, a trail exposed after snowmelt, a fire-watch argument, a sick camp in heat, or animal sign shifting downhill.
Make the page useful for timing scenes and consequences; each season should change movement, prices, visibility, work, wildlife, and what evidence survives.
Keep the language grounded in road dirt, creek height, wind, hay, smoke, frost, dust, timber work, and freight schedules.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: The season should be a foreman nobody hired and everybody obeys.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A seasonal-shift scene should ask what just opened, what just closed, who misjudged the weather, and which sign moved with the animals.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western weather, road, work, and wildlife language only.
The generated page must include image path regions/images/seasonal-shifts.png.
The prompt must not contain final prose, final blockquotes, or finished field marks.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not make seasons abstract, mystical, or purely poetic
do not use modern climate charts, forecasts, phones, vehicles, synthetic fabrics, or tourist-season language
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about seasons as timing pressure, resource pressure, and scene engine.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
